description: A designed public-service infographic (co-branded SAMHSA) in a teal/aqua color scheme. The upper-left bold navy all-caps text reads 'YOUR MENTAL HEALTH MATTERS. KNOW WHERE TO SEEK HELP.' followed by 'PTSD AWARENESS MONTH' with a small teal awareness-ribbon icon. The 'SAMHSA' wordmark is faint at top-right. On the right side is a flat-illustration of a person with long flowing dark hair (the hair forming an abstract dark wave across the upper right) sitting hugging their knees, conveying distress. The body is organized into resource columns with phone and phone+text icons: 'CALL — Veterans Crisis Line 1-800-273-8255 (PRESS 1); National Suicide Prevention Lifeline 1-800-273-TALK (8255); SAMHSA's National Helpline 1-800-662-HELP (4357).' and 'CALL OR TEXT — Disaster Distress Helpline 1-800-985-5990' plus 'VISIT — Behavioral Health Treatment Services Locator FINDTREATMENT.SAMHSA.GOV.' This is a mental-health / PTSD-awareness public service announcement listing crisis hotlines; target audience is the general public, veterans, and disaster survivors. Not immigration-related; produced under the prior administration.
notable_text: YOUR MENTAL HEALTH MATTERS. KNOW WHERE TO SEEK HELP. PTSD AWARENESS MONTH; Veterans Crisis Line 1-800-273-8255; National Suicide Prevention Lifeline 1-800-273-TALK; SAMHSA's National Helpline 1-800-662-HELP; Disaster Distress Helpline 1-800-985-5990; FINDTREATMENT.SAMHSA.GOV
